title: Theory of Computation - P NP NPC NPH - Theoretical/Not Answered

questions.question:
The complexity class \( \textsf{NP} \) corresponds to the class of languages which can be accepted by some nondeterministic Turing machine in polynomial time.

The complexity class \( \textsf{coNP} \) consists of all languages whose complement language is in the class \( \textsf{NP} \).

A complexity class \( \mathcal{C} \) is said to be closed under union if for any two languages \( L_{1} \) and \( L_{2} \) in \( \mathcal{C} \), we also have that \( L_{1} \cup L_{2} \in \mathcal{C} \). Similarly, we also say that a complexity class \( \mathcal{C} \) is \( closed\;under\;intersection \) if for any two languages \( L_{1} \) and \( L_{2} \) in \( \mathcal{C} \), we also have that \( L_{1} \cap L_{2} \in \mathcal{C} \).

Consider the following statements.

  1. The class \( \textsf{NP} \) is closed under union.
  2. The class \( \textsf{coNP} \) is closed under union.
  3. The class \( \textsf{NP} \) is closed under intersection.
  4. The class \( \textsf{coNP} \) is closed under intersection.

Which of the above statements are \( \text{TRUE}? \)



TIFR CSE 2025 | Part B | Question: 6
